title: pittosporum phillyreoides
intro: Pittosporum phillyreoides, also known as the New Zealand tobacco tree or pittonia, is a small evergreen tree or shrub native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat coughs, colds, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antitussive effects. The health benefits of Pittosporum phillyreoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
